case_title: Delayed process with burden shifted back to complainant
decision_trigger: The institution kept the matter active but left further progress dependent on the complainant.
recall_question: What happens when the practical burden of progress is shifted back onto the individual?
desired_outcome: A formal response and procedurally grounded next step

actors.name: Complaints officer
actors.incentive_or_pressure: manage workload and shift practical burden outward
actors.likely_behaviour: keep the process open while leaving momentum with the complainant

evidence_bundle.label: Follow-up correspondence
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows that continued movement depends on complainant input

deadlines.label: Internal response deadline
deadlines.consequence: Missed deadline strengthens the case for external escalation

timeline.label: Initial complaint submitted
timeline.description: Initial complaint was submitted to the institution

timeline.label: Follow-up requested from complainant
timeline.description: Progress is effectively dependent on the complainant supplying further movement

escalation_paths.name: Internal procedural follow-up
escalation_paths.trigger: Process remains active but movement is displaced onto the complainant
escalation_paths.likely_response: Further delay or limited procedural reply

linked_cases.note: Earlier complaint involving delayed institutional movement

structural_insight: The matter appears active, but practical progress is shifted back to the complainant.
personal_positioning: Maintaining a clear chronology while procedural burden is displaced.
decision_note: Hold position, preserve record, and prepare escalation if the deadline passes.
learning_capture: An active case can still place the burden of movement on the individual.
